model.label: MiMo-V2-Flash
model.description: MiMo • Flash-tier model configured through a custom endpoint for fast inference and lightweight agent tasks.

pick.rationale: Florida is a top-5 team nationally with elite efficiency on both ends of the floor, ranking 4th in KenPom and Torvik, while Lehigh, a 284th-ranked Patriot League team, lacks the offensive firepower to compete. The Gators' top-10 adjusted offense and top-5 defense should overwhelm Lehigh's average attack, and there are no significant injury concerns for Florida. Although Lehigh has momentum from its First Four win, the talent gap is too wide for a credible upset path, making Florida the clear favorite to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Analyze Team Quality and Matchup Dynamics
reasoningStep.summary: Florida holds a decisive advantage in all advanced metrics, with a massive gap in offensive and defensive efficiency compared to Lehigh, making an upset highly unlikely.
reasoningStep.evidence: Florida ranks 4th in KenPom and Torvik with an adjusted offensive efficiency of 125.6 (9th) and defensive efficiency of 91.8 (5th), while Lehigh ranks 284th with an adjusted offensive efficiency of 102.8 (290th) and defensive efficiency of 113.1 (unranked). The prior pick for Lehigh in the First Four was based on a narrow edge over Prairie View A&M, but Florida's talent level is far superior.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Florida Gators Scores, Stats and Highlights - ESPN Skip to main content Skip to navigation ESPN NFL NFL NBA NBA NCAAM NCAAM NCAAW NCAAW MLB MLB NHL NHL Soccer Soccer More Sports More Sports Watch Watch Fantasy Fantasy Where to Watch Search 4 Florida Gators 26-7 1st in SEC Home Schedule Statistics Roster Tickets External Link Men's Tournament Challenge NEW: TC Eliminator 2025-26 Schedule Postseason vs TBD 3/20 9:25 pm TNT Regular Season vs 13 Arizona L 93-87 vs North Florida W 104-64 vs Florida St W 78-76 vs Miami W 82-68 vs Merrimack W 80-45 vs TCU L 84-80 vs Providence W 90-78 @ 4 Duke L 67-66 vs 5 UConn L 77-73 vs G Washington W 80-70 vs Saint Francis W 102-61 vs Colgate W 90-60 vs Dartmouth W 94-72 @ Missouri L 76-74 vs 18 Georgia W 92-77 vs 21 Tennessee W 91-67 @ Oklahoma W 96-79 @ 10 Vanderbilt W 98-94 vs LSU W 79-61 vs Auburn L 76-67 @ South Carolina W 95-48 vs 23 Alabama W 100-77 @ Texas A&M W 86-67 @ Georgia W 86-66 vs 25 Kentucky W 92-83 vs South Carolina W 76-62 @ Ole Miss W 94-75 @ Texas W 84-71 vs 20 Arkansas W 111-77 vs Mississippi St W 108-74 @ Kentucky W 84-77 vs Kentucky W 71-63 vs 22 Vanderbilt L 91-74 Full Schedule Gators NCAAM Joe Lunardi's ultimate guide to men's March Madness 2026 20h Joe Lunardi Illustration by ESPN Men's tournament odds: Duke ahead of Michigan, Arizona to win tournament 21h Doug Greenberg Lance King/Getty Images SVPod: Scott Van Pelt and Stanford Steve are here to react to the 2026 NCAA Tournament March Madness bracket. Scott Van Pelt and Stanford Steve are here to react to the 2026 NCAA Tournament March Madness bracket. Steve gives his UNFILTERED thoughts on all the issues with the bracket. Duke's draw is TOUGH, and Steve is giving his Blue Devils NO CHANCE of reaching Indy. A potential Florida-Vandy rematch isn't ideal, and a road game in the Elite 8?? Plus, will chalk indeed fly? Is it Arizona's year? Which upsets does SVP like the most? Plus, a solution to the flopping problem, reaction to Bam Adebayo's 83-point game and an in-depth breakdown of Cam Young's Players Championship victory. Finally, thoughts on the nasty front taking over the east coast, Steve needs tickets to the Women's Final Four, an all-time SVP golf tactic and Steve's admission to grand theft auto. | SVPod Appoximate Time Codes/Topics (0:00) Intro (0:34) Bracket reaction (5:12) Should Miami (OH) have to play in Dayton? (7:37) Conference championship reactions (9:17) Steve’s issues with the bracket (10:42) Should Purdue be a 2-seed? (13:32) More bracket reaction (23:42) Bracketologists nailed it (25:57) The 6-line is LOADED (26:30) Watch out for USF (31:30) UCLA with a BIG road trip ahead (34:20) Which teams can play spoiler? (39:23) Ark-Wisc could be a banger (42:15) ALL the pressure is on Arizona (45:00) Reaction to Bam’s 83 piece (46:45) This year’s freshman class has been GREAT49:10 Final bracket thoughts (55:10) Women’s bracket is a JOKE (57:55) Anybody got tix to Women’s Final

modelTrace.content:
You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.
Bracket config id: 2026-bracket
Tournament year: 2026
Current game id: r64-south-1
Current game label: South 1/16
Round: Round of 64
Resolved teams: Florida (florida) vs Lehigh (lehigh)
Prior committed picks: 20
